text: Here, I am struck by the synthetic glow of technology and design—the sharp lines of glass, steel, and vivid neon demand my attention. The world inside feels curated, almost theatrical. Moving closer, I am compelled by the words on the screen—a reflection on confusion and how perception wavers. I myself feel a bit disoriented, yet curious, as though I am on the threshold of discovery. The R+D wall calls to me: this is a place for exploring ideas, questioning boundaries, and wondering how humans adapt in dizzying times. I feel both small and significant, adrift and anchored, eager to find clarity amid the spectacle.
